description: 2-bladed propeller at 2400 RPM with 20% BPF-frequency (in-phase) thrust variation, modelling periodic loading from a fixed disturbance (strut wake, inflow distortion). Observer 1 m on-axis along thrust direction (z-axis). Mean pressure (static near-field contribution) is removed before OASPL computation.
reference: Farassat & Succi (1980) 'A Review of Propeller Discrete Frequency Noise Prediction Technology', JSV 71(3), Eq. 13 (loading noise, on-axis, BPF harmonic m=1). Cross-checked against Gutin (1948). p_amp = omega_BPF * F_delta / (2*a0*r). F_delta = T0*delta = 2.4 N, omega_BPF = 2*pi*80 Hz, a0 = 340.3 m/s, r = 1 m. p_rms = p_amp/sqrt(2) = 0.1994 Pa, OASPL = 80.0 dB.
model_notes: Cyclic loading is at BPF = B*n_rps (in-phase for all blades), modelling a rotor passing twice per revolution through a fixed aerodynamic disturbance. The FW-H integral also produces a non-radiating static near-field term (r^-2 decay) that must be removed (mean subtracted) before computing far-field OASPL.
